description:
Delete mark-sweep-era unit tests + dead trampoline GC paths to clear the surface for the bump-only Heap rewrite.

Touch:
- src/ir_codegen.rs: delete _legacy_trampoline (#[allow(dead_code)]), delete heap_gc/heap_live_count/heap_freelist_len test helpers (only callers are mark-sweep-era tests being deleted).
- src/ir_codegen.rs: delete tests gc_traces_map_keys_and_values, gc_reclaims_bitstring_when_unrooted, gc_traces_tuple_fields_freeing_pointed_objects_when_outer_dropped, allocate_list_drop_root_gc_reclaims, allocate_list_keep_root_gc_preserves, gc_traces_closure_captured_via_jit, allocate_float_drop_root_gc_reclaims, vec_address_stable_across_gc_via_jit. These encode user-driven mark-sweep semantics that §6.4 (Cheney at park-time) makes invisible.
- src/ir_codegen.rs: delete already-ignored hot_loop_alloc_triggers_safepoint_gc, per_process_gc_threshold_is_independent (in runtime.rs too), heavy_task_gcs_independently_of_lite_task — these encode trampoline-era GC trigger semantics.
- runtime/src/heap.rs: delete collect_roots_from_frame_chain (only called from _legacy_trampoline) and MarkVisitor (only called from gc body about to be deleted).
- src/runtime.rs / src/ir_codegen.rs: rewrite remaining live_count() > 0 sanity asserts to bytes_used() > 0.

Acceptance: full suite green; no references to removed names.
title: fz-cps.GC.1.1: remove mark-sweep-era test surface
